summary: The plan is implementation-ready and faithfully synthesizes all three council drafts against the resolved concept document. Frontmatter carries all required keys (artifact_contract: ce-unified-plan/v1, artifact_readiness: implementation-ready, execution: code, origin pointing at the code-mode synthesis). Guardrail consistency is handled correctly: the managed server keeps exactly one tool (R1/AE1, verified against src/server.ts which registers only search_sessions), and the second native lane is reconciled with the one-tool rule in DESIGN.md/AGENTS.md through an explicit design-amendment unit (U4) gated on maintainer acceptance before the native binary ships — the plan amends the guardrail rather than violating it. FFF remains the sole backend; no custom indexing, embeddings, or stores are introduced. All five fable-specific positions are incorporated: the design-amendment gate (U4, human go/no-go for U5), the four-class policy vocabulary (internal/exposable/denied/unknown in R7 and U2), namespaced mirrored tools (fff_grep/fff_multi_grep, KTD7/AE2), the ship gate on the managed correctness track (R13 plus U6 dependency), and the recorded dissent on the managed re-home (plan line 24 and Alternatives Considered, plus the fable R5 envelope dissent). Kimi contributions (policyVersion + unclassified-tool tripwire, doctor/preflight native check, fff_call meta-tool fallback, re-home as proof-of-abstraction) and codex contributions (ce-plan skeleton, R/AE/KTD IDs, conservative fingerprint canonicalization that preserves arrays exactly, budget constants 256/4/15s/50-200/4MiB, low-level-SDK decision, root-wide coverage disclosure KTD11/AE7, threat model) are all present and accurately attributed in Sources and Research. The six units have concrete files, approaches, patterns, test scenarios, and per-unit verification; the verification contract uses real repo commands; stop conditions and order-changing open questions give the implementer explicit branches. Code-evidence spot checks pass: src/fff-backend.ts listTools is name-only as claimed, package.json ships only three bin entries with no exports, and @modelcontextprotocol/sdk exposes AjvJsonSchemaValidator via ./validation/ajv. A coding agent could implement from this plan without another planning pass.

issues.title: U4 drafting vs. dependency wording is slightly inconsistent
issues.description: Sequencing step 3 says to draft U4 'in parallel with U2–U3', while U4's Dependencies field lists 'U3 (router exists and is proven under managed traffic)'. The intent (draft in parallel, acceptance gates U5) is recoverable from context, so this is not blocking, but an implementer reading only the unit header could serialize U4 behind U3 unnecessarily.
issues.suggestedFix: Rephrase U4's Dependencies line to 'U3 for acceptance; drafting may proceed in parallel with U2–U3' to match the Sequencing section.

feedback: Approve. This is a genuine three-draft synthesis, not a codex draft with fable annotations: the deltas from the 001 plan are real and correctly motivated (restoring codex's conservative array-preserving fingerprint canonicalization over 001's set-like array sorting is the right call, and the restored KTD11 root-wide visibility disclosure closes a genuine safety-documentation gap). The two explicit disagreement resolutions are well-reasoned: taking the managed-lane re-home (2-of-3 drafts plus concept sequencing) while recording fable's dissent and mitigating its blast-radius concern via characterization-first + standalone-revertable-commit + correctness-patch coordination; and choosing the low-level SDK over FastMCP with kimi's meta-tool as a documented fallback behind the U5 spike. The R13 ship gate cleanly resolves the escape-hatch moral hazard without blocking router construction, and U4 makes the one-tool-boundary change a maintainer decision rather than an implementer assumption — exactly how a guardrail-pinned repo should absorb a second MCP surface. Implementation-readiness is strong: budget constants are pinned with boundary tests (call 256 permitted / 257 rejected), every exposure-block state has a stable reason code, the tripwire test forces conscious classification on FFF upgrades, and rollback is per-unit and clean. Only fix worth making before handoff is the trivial U4 dependency wording noted above; it does not require another planning pass.
